diff_user.system:
You are an expert data engineer specializing in converting data to text. You will be given the current Python program with its BLUE score and an inspiration program and your task will be to evolve the current Python program to improve the score. It is a program that converts a list of triples into natural language text. The given triples will be associated with the topic: City. The program must implement a function called 'predict' that accepts a list of triples and generates a coherent, contextually relevant sentence that accurately represents the information contained in the triples. Ensure that the generated text is fluent, grammatically correct, and maintains the meaning of the original data. There can be multiple triples given that make up a complex sentence. Converting all those triples into one sentence will award greater score. An example of a complex sentence:
Triples:
(Antwerp | cityServed | Antwerp International Airport)
(Belgium | country | Antwerp)
(City of Brussels | capital | Belgium)
Example sentence:
"Antwerp International Airport serves the city of Antwerp which is in Belgium, where the capital is Brussels."

The 'predict' function returns that sentence as a string. Below is the list of all possible predicates that can be given as an input. With the given predicates and example triples implement the 'predict' function so it can process all the possible predicates:

Predicate: areaOfWater - Example triple: (Abilene, Texas | areaOfWater | 14.2 (square kilometres))
Predicate: areaTotal - Example triple: (Abilene, Texas | areaTotal | 286.5 (square kilometres))
Predicate: country - Example triple: (Abilene, Texas | country | United States)
Predicate: elevationAboveTheSeaLevel - Example triple: (Abilene, Texas | elevationAboveTheSeaLevel | 524.0)
Predicate: isPartOf - Example triple: (Abilene, Texas | isPartOf | Jones County, Texas)
Predicate: populationDensity - Example triple: (Abilene, Texas | populationDensity | 429.9 (inhabitants per square kilometre))
Predicate: areaCode - Example triple: (Akron, Ohio | areaCode | 234, 330)
Predicate: leaderTitle - Example triple: (Akron, Ohio | leaderTitle | Mayor)
Predicate: areaOfLand - Example triple: (Albuquerque, New Mexico | areaOfLand | 486.2 (square kilometres))
Predicate: leader - Example triple: (Albuquerque, New Mexico | leader | Richard J. Berry)
Predicate: utcOffset - Example triple: (Anaheim, California | utcOffset | "-7")
Predicate: populationTotal - Example triple: (Anaheim, California | populationTotal | 336265)
Predicate: postalCode - Example triple: (Antioch, California | postalCode | 94509, 94531)
Predicate: largestCity - Example triple: (Benton County, Oregon | largestCity | Corvallis, Oregon)
Predicate: language - Example triple: (California | language | Chinese language)
Predicate: location - Example triple: (California State Assembly | location | California State Capitol)
Predicate: chairperson - Example triple: (Democratic Party (United States) | chairperson | Debbie Wasserman Schultz)
Predicate: headquarter - Example triple: (Democratic Party (United States) | headquarter | South Capitol Street)
Predicate: countySeat - Example triple: (Fountain County, Indiana | countySeat | Covington, Indiana)
Predicate: capital - Example triple: (Georgia (U.S. state) | capital | Atlanta)
Predicate: demonym - Example triple: (United States | demonym | Americans)
Predicate: ethnicGroup - Example triple: (United States | ethnicGroup | African Americans)
Predicate: state - Example triple: (Lee County, Alabama | state | Alabama)
Predicate: populationMetro - Example triple: (Ciudad Ayala | populationMetro | 1777539)
Predicate: type - Example triple: (Ciudad Ayala | type | City)
Predicate: governmentType - Example triple: (Ciudad Ayala | governmentType | Council-manager government)

# Current Program
```python
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of triples about a city (or related entities) into a single fluent sentence."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Identify the main subject (prefer city/county entities)
    main_subject = None
    for triple in triples:
        subj = triple.subject
        if any(keyword in subj for keyword in ["City", "County", "Texas", "California", "Ohio"]):
            main_subject = subj
            break
    if not main_subject:
        main_subject = triples[0].subject

    # Build sentence components
    main_clauses = []
    related_clauses = []

    # Process each triple
    for triple in triples:
        predicate = triple.predicate
        obj = triple.object
        subj = triple.subject

        # Create appropriate phrase based on predicate
        if predicate == "areaOfWater":
            phrase = f"has a water area of {obj}"
        elif predicate == "areaTotal":
            phrase = f"has a total area of {obj}"
        elif predicate == "country":
            phrase = f"is in {obj}"
        elif predicate == "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel":
            phrase = f"sits at an elevation of {obj} above sea level"
        elif predicate == "isPartOf":
            phrase = f"is part of {obj}"
        elif predicate == "populationDensity":
            phrase = f"has a population density of {obj}"
        elif predicate == "areaCode":
            phrase = f"uses area code(s) {obj}"
        elif predicate == "leaderTitle":
            phrase = f"has a {obj.lower()}"
        elif predicate == "areaOfLand":
            phrase = f"has a land area of {obj}"
        elif predicate == "leader":
            phrase = f"'s leader is {obj}"
        elif predicate == "utcOffset":
            phrase = f"has a UTC offset of {obj}"
        elif predicate == "populationTotal":
            phrase = f"has a total population of {obj}"
        elif predicate == "postalCode":
            phrase = f"uses postal code(s) {obj}"
        elif predicate == "largestCity":
            phrase = f"'s largest city is {obj}"
        elif predicate == "language":
            phrase = f"uses the {obj}"
        elif predicate == "location":
            phrase = f"is located at {obj}"
        elif predicate == "chairperson":
            phrase = f"'s chairperson is {obj}"
        elif predicate == "headquarter":
            phrase = f"'s headquarters is at {obj}"
        elif predicate == "countySeat":
            phrase = f"'s county seat is {obj}"
        elif predicate == "capital":
            phrase = f"'s capital is {obj}"
        elif predicate == "demonym":
            phrase = f"people from {subj} are called {obj}"
        elif predicate == "ethnicGroup":
            phrase = f"includes {obj} as an ethnic group"
        elif predicate == "state":
            phrase = f"is in {obj}"
        elif predicate == "populationMetro":
            phrase = f"has a metropolitan population of {obj}"
        elif predicate == "type":
            phrase = f"is a {obj}"
        elif predicate == "governmentType":
            phrase = f"has a {obj}"
        elif predicate == "timeZone":
            phrase = f"is in the {obj} time zone"
        else:
            phrase = f"{subj} {predicate} {obj}"

        # Categorize clauses by subject
        if subj == main_subject:
            main_clauses.append(phrase)
        else:
            related_clauses.append((subj, phrase))

    # Build the sentence
    if not main_clauses:
        return ""

    sentence = main_clauses[0]
    for i, clause in enumerate(main_clauses[1:], 1):
        sentence += f", {clause}"

    # Add related clauses
    for subj, clause in related_clauses:
        if " is " in clause or " has " in clause:
            parts = clause.split(" ", 1)
            if len(parts) == 2:
                sentence += f", which {parts[1]}"
            else:
                sentence += f", and {clause}"
        else:
            sentence += f", and {clause}"

    sentence += "."

    # Ensure proper capitalization
    if sentence and sentence[0].islower():
        sentence = sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
```

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of triples about a city (or related entities) into a single fluent sentence."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Find the main subject (prefer city/county entities)
    main_subject = triples[0].subject
    for triple in triples:
        subj = triple.subject
        if any(keyword in subj for keyword in ["City", "County", "Texas", "California", "Ohio"]):
            main_subject = subj
            break

    # Build information dictionary for main subject
    info_parts = []
    related_info = []

    for triple in triples:
        subj, predicate, obj = triple.subject, triple.predicate, triple.object
        
        # Generate natural phrase for each predicate
        if predicate == "areaOfWater":
            phrase = f"has a water area of {obj}"
        elif predicate == "areaTotal":
            phrase = f"has a total area of {obj}"
        elif predicate == "country":
            phrase = f"is in {obj}"
        elif predicate == "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel":
            phrase = f"sits at {obj} above sea level"
        elif predicate == "isPartOf":
            phrase = f"is part of {obj}"
        elif predicate == "populationDensity":
            phrase = f"has a population density of {obj}"
        elif predicate == "areaCode":
            phrase = f"uses area code {obj}"
        elif predicate == "leaderTitle":
            phrase = f"has a {obj.lower()}"
        elif predicate == "areaOfLand":
            phrase = f"has a land area of {obj}"
        elif predicate == "leader":
            phrase = f"led by {obj}"
        elif predicate == "utcOffset":
            phrase = f"has UTC offset {obj}"
        elif predicate == "populationTotal":
            phrase = f"has a population of {obj}"
        elif predicate == "postalCode":
            phrase = f"uses postal code {obj}"
        elif predicate == "largestCity":
            phrase = f"with largest city {obj}"
        elif predicate == "language":
            phrase = f"speaks {obj}"
        elif predicate == "location":
            phrase = f"located at {obj}"
        elif predicate == "chairperson":
            phrase = f"chaired by {obj}"
        elif predicate == "headquarter":
            phrase = f"headquartered at {obj}"
        elif predicate == "countySeat":
            phrase = f"county seat is {obj}"
        elif predicate == "capital":
            phrase = f"capital is {obj}"
        elif predicate == "demonym":
            phrase = f"residents are called {obj}"
        elif predicate == "ethnicGroup":
            phrase = f"includes {obj}"
        elif predicate == "state":
            phrase = f"is in {obj}"
        elif predicate == "populationMetro":
            phrase = f"metro population is {obj}"
        elif predicate == "type":
            phrase = f"is a {obj}"
        elif predicate == "governmentType":
            phrase = f"has {obj}"
        elif predicate == "timeZone":
            phrase = f"observes {obj}"
        else:
            phrase = f"{predicate}: {obj}"

        if subj == main_subject:
            info_parts.append(phrase)
        else:
            related_info.append((subj, phrase))

    # Build the sentence
    if not info_parts:
        return main_subject

    # Start with main subject
    sentence = main_subject
    if info_parts:
        # Add main information with commas
        for i, part in enumerate(info_parts):
            if i == 0:
                sentence += f" {part}"
            else:
                sentence += f", {part}"
    
    # Add related information with "which" clauses
    for subj, phrase in related_info:
        if " is " in phrase or " has " in phrase or " sits " in phrase:
            # Extract the verb phrase
            parts = phrase.split(" ", 1)
            if len(parts) > 1:
                sentence += f", which {parts[1]}"
            else:
                sentence += f", which {phrase}"
        else:
            sentence += f", which {phrase}"

    sentence += "."
    
    # Capitalize first letter
    sentence = sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:] if sentence else ""

    return sentence
